title: Estate agents and auctioneers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Plan, direct, or coordinate the selling, buying, leasing, or governance activities of commercial, industrial, or residential real estate properties. Includes managers of homeowner and condominium associations, rented or leased housing units, buildings, or land (including rights-of-way). Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. (Based on 20 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Act as liaisons between on-site managers or tenants and owners, and negotiate between buyers and sellers over property prices and settlement details during transactions.

Meet with boards of directors and committees to discuss and resolve legal and environmental issues or disputes between neighbors, and confer with legal authorities to ensure compliance with state and federal regulations.

Generate lists of properties for sale, including their locations, descriptions, and available financing options; maintain records of sales, rental or usage activity; and analyze information on property values and market conditions.
Monitor fulfillment of purchase contract terms and check work completed by loan officers, attorneys, or other professionals to ensure quality and compliance with agreements.

Obtain agreements from property owners to place properties for sale, solicit property sales listings, and sell real estate on behalf of clients; prepare documents such as contracts, purchase agreements, and leases.
Prepare and administer contracts for property services, such as cleaning, maintenance, and security, and manage the operational aspects of rental properties.
Confer regularly with community association members to ensure their needs are being met and review property details to ensure environmental regulations are met.
Inspect grounds, facilities, and equipment to determine maintenance needs, and investigate complaints and violations to resolve problems as per management rules.
Conduct market analyses by comparing properties with similar recently sold properties to determine competitive market prices, and assess eligibility of tenants according to government regulations.
Maintain knowledge of real estate law, local economies, fair housing laws, mortgage options, and relevant government programs; advise clients on market conditions, prices, and related matters.
Coordinate the activities of staff and contract personnel to ensure quality management, and prepare budgets and financial reports for properties.
Prepare for property closings by coordinating document signing and disbursement of funds, and act as an intermediary in negotiations for property transactions.
Rent properties on behalf of clients and display properties to prospective tenants while explaining terms and providing information about local areas.
Develop networks of attorneys, mortgage lenders, and contractors for client referrals, and solicit and analyze bids from contractors for property maintenance.

Investigate damage or delays at construction sites and oversee necessary work, ensuring compliance with safety codes and regulations; manage or operate real estate offices and handle associated business details.
Review property listings, trade journals, and literature to remain knowledgeable about real estate markets, and attend relevant seminars and meetings.
Prepare and submit budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports related to property management and development projects.
Promote sales of properties through advertisements and open houses, and accompany buyers during property viewings, advising them on suitability and value.
Conduct seminars and training sessions for agents to improve sales techniques, or negotiate service contracts with clients regarding property management.
Inspect condition and arrangement of premises and notify owners of maintenance needs or arrange necessary repairs, ensuring compliance with environmental standards.
Follow market trends and adjust strategies accordingly to optimize sale prospects or compliance with new regulations that affect property management.
Advise clients on financial and legal matters, such as investments and taxes.

Analyze blueprints and other documentation to prepare time, cost, materials, and labor estimates, and prepare cost and expenditure statements and documentation at regular intervals for the duration of the project.
Arrange meetings concerning issues involving their clients and consult with clients to develop strategies for their careers, and explain actions taken on their behalf.
Attend sales meetings, industry trade shows, and training seminars to gather information, promote products, and expand network of contacts.
Identify new advertising markets and propose products to serve them, and prepare promotional plans, sales literature, media kits, and sales contracts using computer.
Conduct auditions or interviews to evaluate potential clients and visit sites to record information about access, drainage, topography, and availability of utility services.
Gather all relevant material for bid processes, coordinate bidding and contract approval, and establish and maintain tendering processes and conduct negotiations.
Prepare estimates for use in selecting vendors or subcontractors and prepare estimates used by management for purposes such as planning, organizing, and scheduling work.
Communicate with clients, vendors, and personnel to discuss and formulate estimates and resolve issues.
Negotiate with managers, promoters, and union officials regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations, and collect fees, commissions, or other payments according to contract terms.
Create business continuity and disaster recovery budgets, prepare reports summarizing operational results, and analyze impact on and risk to essential business functions or information systems.
Keep informed of industry trends and deals, and develop contacts with individuals and organizations to apply effective strategies and techniques for clients' success.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to new and existing customers, and explain to customers how specific types of advertising will help promote their products or services effectively.
Request and process all correspondence and paperwork related to accounts, and create or maintain a database of customer accounts.
Collect payment for merchandise and services and order or purchase merchandise to maintain optimal inventory levels.
Create or distribute offline promotional materials such as brochures, pamphlets, and business cards.
Confer with engineers, architects, owners, contractors, and subcontractors on changes to cost estimates and prepare documentation for case reports or court proceedings.
Conduct or oversee contingency plan integration and operation, and develop or revise business plans for online business.
Initiate online auctions through auction hosting sites and implement security practices to preserve assets and ensure customer privacy.
Research or review regulatory, technical, or market issues related to sustainability, and identify or investigate violations of environmental policies.

Identify or create new sustainability indicators, assess or propose sustainability initiatives considering cost effectiveness and technical feasibility, and develop reports to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives.

Write grant applications, rebate applications, or project proposals to secure funding for sustainability projects and provide technical or administrative support for sustainability programs or issues.
Identify or procure needed resources to implement sustainability programs or projects, and create or maintain plans or documents related to these projects.
Create marketing or outreach media to communicate sustainability issues and collect information about waste stream management to inform decision makers.
Purchase items for resale via online or physical sources, determine location for product listings, and process payments from customers using electronic transaction services.
Write reports to summarize testing activities, prepare or organize online storefront marketing material, and compose product descriptions for online storefronts, auction sites, or shopping websites.
Calculate revenue, sales, and expenses using financial accounting software, keep records pertaining to purchases and sales, and prepare sales and inventory reports for management.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales and inventory-taking, and monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information, plan work schedules, assign employees to specific duties, and provide assistance in performing complicated tasks.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel and confer with company officials to develop methods for increasing sales and expanding markets.
Formulate pricing policies on merchandise according to profitability requirements and address customer complaints regarding services or products.

Remove irrelevant tasks regarding project management and sustainability project specifics not applicable to estate agents and auctioneers, including negotiation, monitoring, and planning project activities.
Examine merchandise for correct pricing and display, and inventory stock, reordering when necessary, to promote sales effectively.
Prepare rental or lease agreements, specifying charges and payment procedures for use of machinery or other items, and coordinate sales promotion activities.
